sections.title: Programming Languages
sections.description: The solid foundations upon which I build logic and digital ecosystems.

items.title: go
items.description: An open-source programming language developed by Google, renowned for its efficiency, concurrency management, and fast execution speed.

items.title: html
items.description: The fundamental markup language used to structure and format content for web pages.

items.title: javascript
items.description: A vital client-side and server-side scripting language used to make websites interactive and dynamic.

items.title: php
items.description: A widely-used server-side programming language designed for web development and creating dynamic web applications.

items.title: css
items.description: The style sheet language used to define the formatting, design, and visual layout of HTML documents.

items.title: sass
items.description: A CSS extension and preprocessor that introduces advanced features like variables, loops, and nesting to stylesheet authoring.

items.title: bootstrap
items.description: One of the world's most popular CSS and JavaScript frameworks for building responsive and mobile-first web interfaces.

items.title: flutter
items.description: Google's UI toolkit for crafting natively compiled applications for mobile, web, and desktop from a single codebase.

items.title: nextjs

items.title: react
items.description: A JavaScript library for building component-based, highly reactive, and reusable user interfaces.

items.title: svelte
items.description: An innovative JavaScript framework that shifts work to compile-time, producing ultra-lightweight native code.

items.title: symfony
items.description: A high-performance, structured PHP framework ideal for building complex enterprise-level web applications.

items.title: tailwind
items.description: A utility-first CSS framework that allows developers to design custom user interfaces directly within the HTML code.

items.title: mongodb
items.description: A document-oriented NoSQL database that stores data in flexible, JSON-like formats.

items.title: mysql
items.description: One of the most popular open-source relational database management systems (RDBMS) in the world.

items.title: phpmyadmin

items.title: plesk
items.description: A web hosting platform and control panel for simplified server configuration and website management.

items.description: A software library providing a lightweight, self-contained, and serverless relational database engine.

items.title: wordpress
items.description: The world's most popular content management system (CMS) used to build and manage websites and blogs.

about.aboutMeContent1: My name is Riccardo Buzzolo, I was born in Cattolica (RN) on December 13, 1994. I have a solid technical background, starting from the
about.aboutMeContent3: studies at the University of Bologna (Cesena campus). My professional experience began with mobile development at Mango Mobile and operational analysis at Teddy Group. I then moved into a full-stack role at
about.aboutMeContent4: , where I worked on the development of management software and online booking platforms for the tourism sector nationwide. I am currently a developer at Quadra S.r.l., focusing on backend development with Symfony (PHP), custom WordPress solutions, and cross-platform mobile apps using Flutter. My goal is to keep expanding my technical toolkit to handle all phases of a project—from database architecture to the final UI—ensuring reliable and functional software for the end user.
about.aboutMyWorkContent2: , I specialize in transforming business requirements into tangible digital solutions, managing the entire software lifecycle from initial architecture to final deployment. My approach goes beyond mere coding; it is rooted in software engineering aimed at solving complex problems through elegant, scalable technology. My workflow is built upon three fundamental pillars, each selected to ensure maximum performance and maintainability:
about.aboutMyWorkContent4:
(PHP):
I leverage the Symfony Framework to build robust web applications and API-first architectures. By utilizing Symfony’s modular components, I handle complex business logic while adhering to SOLID principles and ensuring seamless data persistence via Doctrine ORM. This enables the creation of bespoke platforms tailored for high-demand enterprise environments.
about.aboutMyWorkContent6:
:
For projects requiring dynamic content control, I implement high-end WordPress solutions. Rather than relying on off-the-shelf templates, I focus on custom theme development and bespoke plugin engineering. This ensures the CMS is perfectly aligned with the client’s operational workflow while maintaining peak performance, rigorous security standards, and SEO optimization.
about.aboutMyWorkContent8:
:
On the mobile front, I utilize Flutter, Google’s UI toolkit, to build high-performance native applications for both iOS and Android from a single Dart codebase. Thanks to its declarative UI approach and widget-based architecture, I deliver fluid user experiences and accelerated time-to-market, integrating complex hardware features and real-time data synchronization.
